# Repository Instructions for GitHub Copilot

## Commit Message Format

All commits must follow this format:

```
<type> <emoji>: <short description>
```

**Example:** `fix 🐛: resolve incorrect bearing calculation in lidar_geometry`

**Rules:**
- The description should be concise and written in the imperative mood (e.g., "add", "fix", "remove", not "added", "fixed", "removed")
- Always include the emoji after the type
- Keep the description under 72 characters when possible

## Commit Types and Emojis

| Type | Emoji | When to use |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `feat` | ✨ | Introduce a new feature |
| `fix` | 🐛 | Fix a bug |
| `hotfix` | 🚑️ | Critical hotfix |
| `docs` | 📝 | Add or update documentation |
| `style` | 💄 | Improve code structure or formatting (no logic change) |
| `refactor` | ♻️ | Refactor code without fixing a bug or adding a feature |
| `perf` | ⚡️ | Improve performance |
| `test` | ✅ | Add, update, or fix tests |
| `config` | 🔧 | Add or update configuration files |
| `deps` | 🚼 | Dependency change |
| `chore` | 🔨 | Add or update development scripts or tooling |
| `move` | 🚚 | Move or rename files or paths |
| `types` | 🏷️ | Add or update type annotations |
| `log` | 🔊 | Update logs |
| `cleanup` | 🗑️ | Remove code or files |
| `comments` | 💡 | Add or update comments in source code |
| `gitignore` | 🙈 | Add or update .gitignore |
| `catch` | 🥅 | Catch errors |

For more details, see [.github/commit-instructions.md](.github/commit-instructions.md).
